mergeRationale: The patch only special-cases the one advisory example (dash-separated /:a-:b); the same overlapping-capture ReDoS survives for other separators, custom-pattern params, and repeated modifiers.

requiredFindings.summary: The patch special-cases one adjacent-parameter shape (dash separator) but does not remove the general overlapping-capture ReDoS.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Only token.suffix === '-' is tightened. Routes with other separators (e.g. /:a.:b, /:a_:b), custom-pattern captures, or repeat/optional modifiers still compile to overlapping ([^/]+?)<sep>([^/]+?) regexes that backtrack on long inputs and block the Node event loop.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Fix the compiler so every adjacent capture has an unambiguous boundary for any separator (or emit atomic/possessive-style patterns), or upgrade to a patched path-to-regexp version.

requiredFindings.summary: The added test only exercises the one route shape that was fixed.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It covers /:a-:b with a dash separator; other separators, custom captures and repeated params are untested, so the suite stays green while the ReDoS class remains.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Add timed regression tests for other separators, custom-pattern params, repeated/optional modifiers, and long non-matching inputs.

optionalFindings.summary: When the new branch fires it returns [^/-]+? and ignores token.pattern, so /:id(\d+)-:x silently loses its custom constraint.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because the added ReDoS test passes.
Bounding /:a-:b at the dash is enough because that is the pattern in the advisory.
